build_from.reason: Holds the finding-merge rules (Merge, mergeSeverity, modalCategory, mergeEvidence, confidenceFor), the Merged struct that embeds stream.Finding + Disagreement + *Verification (the exact entanglement the epic flagged), the mergeVerification + verdictRank verify-precedence logic, and the package-local SeverityRank copy. It is the heart of the reconciler and the central extraction target.
build_from.suggested_approach: Split this file along the public/private boundary: the public types (Merged, Verification, JSONFinding/Finding, Verdict constants) and the pure merge logic move into the new reconcile/ library module (package reconcile, stdlib-only). The path-validation merge (mergePathFields / PathValid/PathWarning/PathSuggestion) stays behind in an ATCR adapter because those fields are ATCR-internal. The library's own Finding type replaces both stream.Finding (input) and the path-stripped JSONFinding (output); ATCR adapts stream.Finding <-> reconcile.Finding at the boundary.

semantic_matches.relevance: Public entry point Reconcile(sources []Source, opts Options) Result + the Options/Result/Summary types. Lifted as-is per AC#3.
Verify-precedence merge (confirmed>unverifiable>refuted, skeptic provenance unioned). Travels with the library because it is embedded in Merged and used inside the merge algorithm.

Defines Verification (line 40), JSONFinding (line 74), and VerdictConfirmed/Refuted/Unverifiable (61-63) ALONGSIDE the file-I/O emit layer. This mixing of public types with I/O is the core extraction split.
Defines the public Source type alongside the findings.txt file-discovery I/O. Type moves to library; the Discover() file reading stays ATCR-internal.
Token-set Jaccard dedupe with integer cross-multiplication (exact 0.7/0.4 thresholds). Pure (only stream.Finding type dep). Moves to library. Does NOT use levenshtein.
Canonical severity rubric + normalizer. Pure (strings only). Moves into the library as the canonical owner; reconcile's local SeverityRank copy (merge.go:30) then sources from the library.

Pure edit-distance. BUT currently used ONLY by ATCR-internal path validation (stream/suggest.go), NOT by dedupe. Moving it to the library adds an internal/stream -> library import dependency with no core benefit. Flag as Phase-0 scope question (clarification Q5 was 85% confident).
Disagreement radar projection over merged findings + gray-zone clusters. Uses JSONFinding + Verdict constants. Moves to library.
Confidence-from-verdict rule and v2 confidence ordinal. Consumes VerdictConfirmed/Refuted. Moves to library; verdictRank lives in merge.go.
ATCR-INTERNAL gate predicates. Consumes VerdictRefuted/VerdictConfirmed from Merged.Verification. Stays in ATCR; imports the library's now-public Verification + Verdict constants.
Deterministic (FILE, LINE±3) single-linkage location clustering. Pure; moves to library.
Gray-zone sidecar content-addressed IDs and hash. Moves to library with AmbiguousCluster.
Shared fix-attribution constants used by verify and ghaction. Moves to library so the producer/consumer contract stays compile-time visible.
ATCR-INTERNAL Epic 5.0/5.4 path validation. Stays in ATCR; stamps PathValid/PathWarning/PathSuggestion onto Merged.Finding after Reconcile returns.
Part of the test corpus that proves no behavioral change (AC#3). Pure-logic tests move with the library; I/O/gate/validate/pathsuggest tests stay ATCR-internal.
Documents the wire format the JSON adapter (AC#4) must round-trip: findings.txt stream + reconciled/findings.json + ambiguous.json + disagreements.json sidecars.
CLI reconcile entry point. After extraction it calls the ATCR-internal RunReconcile wrapper; the wrapper adapts stream.Finding -> reconcile.Finding and retains path-validation fields.
One-shot review -> reconcile wiring. Calls reconcile.RunReconcile and reads back reconciled findings for verify/debate/gate. Boundary adapter site.
Resume path calls reconcile.RunReconcile after finishing pending agents. Another boundary adapter consumer.
MCP atcr_reconcile handler. Must import the library's public types and/or the ATCR boundary adapter; shares gate semantics with CLI.

Cross-examination reads/updates reconciled findings and ambiguous clusters. Imports reconcile types; after extraction it uses the library's public Verification/Verdict/DisagreementItem/AmbiguousCluster.
Writes verdicts into findings[i].Verification. After extraction writes into the library's public Verification type.
Aggregates per-skeptic Verification blocks. After extraction operates on library-owned Verification.
Renders disagreements from library types (DisagreementsFile/JSONFinding). Reference-only; imports library.
GitHub Action renders check-run output from reconcile.JSONFinding and uses reconcile.IsFailing. Imports library types.
Uses stream.NormalizeSeverity/SeverityRank for fan-out metrics. After severity moves to the library, this package imports the library.
Uses stream.NormalizeSeverity/SeverityRank for min_severity/max_findings guardrails. After severity moves to the library, this package imports the library.
Current leaderboard/scorecard methodology doc. Must be updated to cite github.com/samestrin/atcr/reconcile as the reference implementation (AC#8).

existing_patterns.name: Self-contained reconcile package
existing_patterns.description: internal/reconcile is already a black-box package: one public entry (Reconcile) + public types (Source/Merged/Result/Options/Summary) + private helpers. Imports only internal/stream.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The library extraction is mostly a mechanical move; preserve the public surface and helper layout.

existing_patterns.name: Independent SeverityRank copy
existing_patterns.description: merge.go:30 copies stream.SeverityRank at package init so mutations to one map cannot corrupt the other.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Once severity moves into the library, the library owns the canonical map; eliminate the redundant copy (or have stream copy from the library).

existing_patterns.name: Deterministic total-order output
existing_patterns.description: sortMerged orders by severity desc, then file, then line so the same input yields byte-identical artifacts — required for a deterministic CI gate.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Preserve the sort exactly; it is what makes the reconciler a credible reference implementation.

existing_patterns.name: Integer-cross-multiply thresholds
existing_patterns.description: classify uses inter*10 vs union*N instead of float comparison so the 0.7/0.4 boundaries are exact and never float-rounding-dependent.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Keep integer comparisons; do not introduce float-based thresholds in the library.

existing_patterns.name: Input vs wire type split
existing_patterns.description: stream.Finding is the per-source input; JSONFinding is the reconciled wire form carrying Reviewers list + path-validation fields + Verification + ClusterMerged.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Library Finding unifies the core wire fields; ATCR JSONFinding wraps/embeds it + path fields at the adapter boundary.

existing_patterns.name: Library-type consumers across ATCR
existing_patterns.description: internal/debate, internal/verify, internal/report, internal/ghaction, internal/mcp, and internal/fanout all consume reconcile types or severity helpers. After extraction they import github.com/samestrin/atcr/reconcile.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Use the library's public API everywhere; avoid re-declaring verdict/severity constants in ATCR packages.

integration_points.description: Public pipeline entry. Lifted as-is; Source.Findings becomes []reconcile.Finding (library-owned) and ATCR converts stream.Finding at the boundary.
Merged embeds *Verification. gate.go (ATCR) and internal/debate both read/mutate it. Verification becomes public library API; consumers import the library.
Debate writes verdicts into findings[i].Verification. Stays ATCR-internal; operates on the library's public Verification via import.
Verify aggregates per-skeptic Verification blocks. After extraction uses the library's public Verification type.

CLI constructs []Source and calls Reconcile (via RunReconcile). This is the ATCR boundary adapter site: stream.Finding -> reconcile.Finding conversion, path-validation fields retained in ATCR JSONFinding.
MCP atcr_reconcile handler shares the same boundary adapter and gate semantics with the CLI.
GitHub Action check-run conclusion keys off reconcile.JSONFinding.Verification and reconcile.IsFailing.
Fan-out metrics and guardrails use NormalizeSeverity/SeverityRank. After severity moves to the library these packages import it.
Leaderboard methodology must cite the extracted library as the reference implementation.

integration_gaps.description: docs/findings-format.md defines atcr-findings/v1 text and reconciled JSON, but the standalone external JSON stream the adapter must accept/emit is not yet specified. Need a simple per-source JSON schema (Severity/File/Line/Problem/Fix/Category/EstMinutes/Evidence/Reviewer) and a reconciled output schema aligned with library Result.
integration_gaps.resolution: Define schema family 'reconcile-json/v1', versioned INDEPENDENTLY of atcr-findings/v1. INPUT (decode -> []reconcile.Source): a JSON object per source {"version":"reconcile-json/v1","source":"<name>","findings":[{severity,file,line,problem,fix,category,est_minutes,evidence,reviewer}]}; the adapter accepts either a single object or an array of these objects, mapping each to one reconcile.Source whose Findings are []reconcile.Finding. OUTPUT (encode reconcile.Result): {"version":"reconcile-json/v1","reconciled_at":<RFC3339>,"findings":[{severity,file,line,problem,fix,category,est_minutes,evidence,reviewers:[],confidence,disagreement?,verification?{verdict,skeptic,notes}}],"summary":{...},"ambiguous":[...]}. Field names are the library Finding's JSON tags; path-validation fields (PathValid/PathWarning/PathSuggestion/ClusterMerged) are ATCR-internal and NOT part of the external schema. omitempty on disagreement/verification so absence is byte-stable. Additive-only evolution within v1, mirroring the atcr-findings policy.

integration_gaps.description: levenshtein.go is pure but used only by ATCR path validation. Phase 0 must decide whether to move+export it into the library (adding an internal/stream -> library import) or leave it in internal/stream.
integration_gaps.resolution: LEAVE in internal/stream. Code audit confirms levenshtein/similarity is consumed ONLY by internal/stream/suggest.go (ATCR path validation); dedupe.go uses token-set Jaccard, NOT edit distance. Moving it would add a stream -> library import with zero reconciler benefit. This reverses clarification Q5's 85%-confidence 'move' lean on the basis of grep evidence (ratified by maintainer 2026-06-23). Drop reconcile/levenshtein.go from files_to_create.

integration_gaps.description: ATCR currently calls reconcile.RunReconcile, which reads stream.Finding from disk and returns Merged with path-validation fields. After extraction, a dedicated adapter must convert []stream.Finding -> []reconcile.Finding before calling library Reconcile and wrap results back into an ATCR-internal JSONFinding. The exact package (internal/reconcile/adapter, internal/reconcileio, or inline in gate.go/emit.go) is TBD.
integration_gaps.resolution: Use internal/reconcile/adapter (new subpackage). Ratified by maintainer 2026-06-23. Keeps the boundary conversion beside the ATCR-internal gate.go/validate.go that stay behind, isolates stream.Finding <-> reconcile.Finding conversion + path-validation stamping + file I/O in one package, and gives a clean import path. Create internal/reconcile/adapter/adapter.go.

integration_gaps.description: internal/debate, internal/verify, internal/report, internal/ghaction, internal/mcp, and internal/fanout all reference reconcile types or severity helpers. Each must be updated to import github.com/samestrin/atcr/reconcile after the move.
integration_gaps.resolution: Audit complete (grep 2026-06-23). Non-test consumers of internal/reconcile types: cmd/atcr (github.go, reconcile.go, report.go, resume.go, review.go, verify.go), internal/debate, internal/ghaction, internal/mcp, internal/report, internal/verify, AND internal/scorecard/reconcile.go (NOT previously listed). Severity-helper (NormalizeSeverity/SeverityRank) consumers outside stream: internal/fanout (metrics.go, postprocess.go), internal/debate (envelope.go, select.go), internal/verify/severity.go, internal/report/render.go, AND internal/registry/config.go (NOT previously listed). Add internal/scorecard and internal/registry to the import-flip work item; severity scope is broader than fanout-only.

integration_gaps.description: docs/leaderboard-methodology.md does not exist; docs/scorecard.md is the current leaderboard/scorecard doc. It must be updated to cite github.com/samestrin/atcr/reconcile as the reference implementation (AC#8).
integration_gaps.resolution: Target confirmed: docs/scorecard.md (docs/leaderboard-methodology.md does not exist; scorecard.md feeds the Epic 10.0 Model-Eval Leaderboard, line 11). The citation edit is DEFERRED to the implementation phase (it must reference the module after it exists, not during Phase-0 planning). Planned text: a note that the deterministic reconciler backing every scorecard record is the standalone reference implementation github.com/samestrin/atcr/reconcile.

integration_gaps.description: Existing .github/workflows/ci.yml tests only the root module. The new reconcile-module.yml must run on tag push (AC#7) and should probably also be invoked from the root CI on PRs to prevent regressions.
integration_gaps.resolution: Dual coverage (ratified by maintainer 2026-06-23). (1) New .github/workflows/reconcile-module.yml triggers on tag push for the release gate (AC#7): cd ./reconcile && gofmt check + golangci-lint + go test -race. (2) Add a PR/push job (or step) in ci.yml that cds into ./reconcile and runs go test, because the root 'go test ./...' does NOT cross the nested module's go.mod boundary, so the library would otherwise be untested between tags. Both reuse the existing self-hosted [gauntlet] runner and Go 1.25 setup from ci.yml.

architecture_notes: CORE ENTANGLEMENT: emit.go and discover.go each MIX public types (Verification, JSONFinding, Verdict consts, Source) with ATCR-INTERNAL file I/O. The extraction's central Phase-0 task is splitting each: public types + pure logic move to reconcile/ library; file I/O + path-validation stays in an ATCR adapter package.

TWO FINDING TYPES: stream.Finding (per-source input) vs JSONFinding (reconciled wire form with path fields). The library defines ONE Finding carrying the core wire fields (Severity, File, Line, Problem, Fix, Category, EstMinutes, Evidence, Reviewer(s), Confidence) + Disagreement + Verification. ATCR's JSONFinding embeds/wraps it and adds PathValid/PathWarning/PathSuggestion/ClusterMerged.

SeverityRank already has an independent copy in merge.go:30 (copied from stream at init). Once severity moves into the library, the library owns the canonical map; eliminate the redundant copy or have internal/stream source it from the library.

SCOPE AMBIGUITY (flag): levenshtein.go is pure BUT currently used only by ATCR-internal path validation (stream/suggest.go), not by dedupe (which uses Jaccard). Moving it to the library adds an internal/stream -> library import dependency with no core benefit. Clarification Q5 chose to move it at 85% confidence; revisit in Phase 0 (either move+export Levenshtein, or leave it in stream).

Verdict constants (VerdictConfirmed/Refuted/Unverifiable) are already exported; gate.go (ATCR-internal), confidence.go, disagree.go, merge.go all use them. They move to the library unchanged; gate.go imports them. No visibility change.

CONSUMERS that touch reconcile types and must re-import the library: internal/debate (mutates Verification), internal/verify (aggregates Verification), internal/report (renders disagreements), internal/ghaction (gates on Verification/IsFailing), internal/mcp (reconcile handler), internal/fanout (NormalizeSeverity/SeverityRank), cmd/atcr (gate + reconcile wiring). Path-validation fields (PathValid/PathWarning/PathSuggestion) stay ATCR-internal in the adapter.

SEQUENCING: epics 13.0 (semantic NCD dedup), 13.2 (bipartite DBSCAN), 13.3 (PageRank confidence) plan to REPLACE the dedup/confidence algorithms. Extraction must land BEFORE them (matches the epic's 'stable surface' dependency note); those epics then modify the extracted library.

NESTED MODULE: ./reconcile/ with its own go.mod, consumed via root go.mod replace github.com/samestrin/atcr/reconcile => ./reconcile. Separate-repo publication follows extraction, does not precede it.

LIFT-AS-IS: keep Reconcile(sources []Source, opts Options) Result and Options{ReconciledAt, Partial, Merges, Root} exactly. The epic's proposed clean API ((*Result, error), ReconciledFinding, Options{LineTolerance, SimilarityThreshold}) is DEFERRED to a follow-on epic.

LEADERBOARD DOC: docs/leaderboard-methodology.md does not exist. docs/scorecard.md is the current scorecard/leaderboard methodology doc and is the correct place to add the reference-implementation citation (AC#8).
architecture_recommendations: Keep the reconcile/ module strictly stdlib-only in non-test files; testify and other test helpers are allowed only in *_test.go files.

Lift the public API exactly as-is: Reconcile(sources []Source, opts Options) Result, Source, Merged, Options{ReconciledAt, Partial, Merges, Root}, Result, Summary. Defer the epic's proposed clean-API reshape to a follow-on epic.
Make the library the canonical owner of SeverityRank and NormalizeSeverity; have internal/stream re-export or copy from the library so fanout/reconcile cannot drift.

Introduce a single ATCR boundary adapter (e.g., internal/reconcile/adapter/adapter.go) responsible for stream.Finding <-> reconcile.Finding conversion and for wrapping library Result back into the ATCR-internal JSONFinding with path-validation fields.
Define the external JSON adapter schema in reconcile/adapter/json before implementing the adapter; keep it simple and versioned independently from atcr-findings/v1.
Root go.mod should use replace github.com/samestrin/atcr/reconcile => ./reconcile during development; remove or update the replace only when publishing the library to a separate repository.
Update docs/scorecard.md to cite github.com/samestrin/atcr/reconcile as the reference implementation, satisfying AC#8 without creating a new docs/leaderboard-methodology.md file.

Add nested-module test coverage in .github/workflows/reconcile-module.yml triggered on tag push, and consider invoking it from .github/workflows/ci.yml on every PR so the library never breaks silently.
